events.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Following Strikes
events.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz after an unapproved vessel was struck. This action follows a series of escalations and U.S. strikes on Iranian port cities, escalating tensions in the critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US Strikes Iranian Port Cities, IRGC Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Explosions were reported in multiple Iranian port cities, including Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Chabahar, Bandar-e Deyr, and Asaluyeh, as U.S. military operations continued. In response, the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz closed.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes After Tanker Attacks
events.desc: The U.S. military reported striking Iranian missile sites and command centers, while Iran claimed to have attacked U.S. bases. These retaliatory actions occurred in the aftermath of attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Vows Revenge for Father's Killing
events.desc: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei pledged retaliation for the U.S.-Israeli killing of his father and predecessor. This statement follows escalating rhetoric and military exchanges between Iran and the U.S. after the interim agreement aiming to end the conflict collapsed.

events.title: US Issues Fresh Sanctions on Iran Amid Escalating Conflict
events.desc: The U.S. Treasury Department imposed new sanctions on Iran, targeting Ali Ansari, an Iranian banker and businessman, along with 13 other individuals and entities. These measures follow Tehran's renewed attacks on oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Demands Iran Stop Attacks in Hormuz Strait
events.desc: U.S. officials stated that the United States is demanding Iran publicly commit to halting attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz and ensure all lanes remain open. Iran has resisted relinquishing control of the vital waterway.

events.title: Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran
events.desc: President Trump indicated that the U.S. would likely conduct further strikes against Iran, stating that any retaliatory actions would be swift. This comes amid heightened tensions and exchanges of fire following the attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Bombs Iranian Port Cities as IRGC Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US conducted strikes on port cities including Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Chabahar, Bandar-e Deyr and Asaluyeh. Following these attacks, the IRGC announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit route.

events.title: Iran Nuclear and Military Damage Revealed
events.desc: Restricted satellite imagery has revealed significant damage to Iran's nuclear facilities, ballistic missile infrastructure, and naval bases following recent U.S. strikes.

events.title: US Launches Fresh Strikes as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. military launched new strikes in response to an attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Iran subsequently announced the closure of the vital waterway.

events.title: Mediators Push US-Iran Talks in Qatar and Oman
events.desc: Regional mediators are intensifying efforts to prevent further escalation between the United States and Iran by facilitating discussions in Qatar and Oman.

events.title: US Seeks Free Hormuz Access from Iran
events.desc: The U.S. is seeking a public commitment from Iran regarding the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Discussions between Iranian and Omani officials addressed arrangements for transit, as Washington pushes for secure access.

events.title: Iran Strikes Back at Trump After Truce Claim
events.desc: Iran asserted that it upheld its commitment to a ceasefire with the United States, responding to President Trump's declaration that the truce was over. This exchange occurred despite the prior signing of a memorandum of understanding aimed at peace.

events.title: US Extends Work Permits for Immigrants with Temporary Protected Status
events.desc: The Trump administration extended work permits for hundreds of thousands of immigrants from Haiti and six other countries, including Syria and Yemen. These extensions were granted just before their original expiration dates.

events.title: US Makes it Easier to Export AI Chips and Military Equipment to UAE
events.desc: The U.S. has eased export controls for the United Arab Emirates, facilitating the transfer of Nvidia AI chips, military hardware, and other advanced technologies. This move strengthens bilateral relations amid Washington's strategy towards Iran.
